title: Create LinkedIn post generation workflow
summary: Create a workflow for generating LinkedIn posts from arbitrary selected context scopes, including foundry-level context, one project, multiple projects, one task, multiple tasks, or whole-system context, while preserving source boundaries and requiring human approval before any publishing action.
acceptance_criteria: the workflow accepts arbitrary selected context scopes and preserves provenance for each included project, task, or system source
the first version is draft-only and distinguishes local draft generation from external LinkedIn publishing
the workflow produces multiple candidate post drafts with distinct angles before any final selection
the workflow includes a post-level grilling step that captures the user's intent, framing, and wording before drafting
the workflow treats the user's wording during grilling as voice source material for matching style without fabricating claims
the workflow creates and reuses a durable voice profile for the user while still allowing current-run wording to influence each draft
the workflow only updates the reusable voice profile after explicit manual approval
each candidate draft includes a provenance block showing the projects, tasks, and context claims that informed it
candidate drafts may include personal reflection, but source-backed claims and user reflections are labeled separately
candidate drafts optimize primarily for the user's personal credibility, with project or workflow usefulness used as supporting evidence
candidate drafts use a humble builder voice by default, showing judgment, process, and proof without generic authority-posturing
candidate drafts avoid emojis and hashtags by default unless the user explicitly asks for them
candidate drafts prefer specific implementation details over broad strategic narrative while explaining why the details matter
each candidate draft includes quality scores for credibility, voice match, evidence/provenance, specificity, and LinkedIn readability
the workflow recommends one winning draft with rationale after generating and scoring candidate drafts
the workflow runs a red-team pass for cringe, overclaiming, vague bragging, and unsupported hype before recommending a winning draft
each draft run requires a target audience during grilling rather than silently inferring audience from selected context
each draft run asks for a low, medium, or high personalness level during grilling
draft runs do not require a desired reader action, though the workflow may suggest one when useful
the workflow produces short-form LinkedIn drafts only, not long-form variants
short-form drafts target 900-1300 characters with a hard maximum around 1500 characters
the workflow supports editing an existing rough draft as an input path in addition to generating drafts from context plus grilling
completed draft runs are stored as durable project artifacts instead of existing only in chat output
the task records how source project or whole-system context is loaded without mutating source project trackers by default
the task identifies fixture or eval examples that prove generated posts match the intended audience and evidence boundaries
the task reaches implementation only after Matt Pocock grilling, PRD, and issue shaping are complete

context_snapshot.summary: Load the LinkedIn Posts project state, setup tracker task, and this task before grilling the LinkedIn post generation workflow.

approved_artifacts.approval_note: User explicitly asked to save the current LinkedIn health insurance draft now.

session_log.event: Created intake task after the user invoked initiate-task for the new LinkedIn Posts project.
Moved task into Matt Pocock grilling after the user invoked continue-task and explicitly asked to go into grilling.

Resolved first grilling decision: the primary workflow is draft-only. It may produce LinkedIn post drafts and provenance, but posting, scheduling, or external LinkedIn writes are out of scope unless a later approval-gated capability is created.

Resolved scope decision: draft runs are not constrained to one default scope. A run may include any selected scope, including multiple projects and multiple tasks; the invariant is source-level provenance and boundary preservation.
Resolved draft-shape decision: the workflow should produce a set of candidate drafts with different angles before final selection.

Added post-level grilling requirement: each draft run should interview the user about intent and wording, then use the user's wording as voice source material so drafts better match the user's style while staying evidence-bound.
Resolved voice-memory decision: the workflow should create and reuse a durable voice profile for the user, with current-run wording still available as fresh style input for each draft.
Resolved voice-profile update rule: reusable voice profile changes require explicit manual approval. Draft runs may propose updates, but they must not silently mutate the profile.
Resolved provenance-output decision: every candidate draft should include a provenance block naming the projects, tasks, and context claims that informed the draft.
Resolved reflection-boundary decision: drafts may include unsupported personal reflection, but must label reflection separately from source-backed claims.
Resolved audience-goal decision: candidate drafts should optimize primarily for the user's personal credibility, using project or workflow usefulness as supporting evidence.
Resolved default voice direction: candidate drafts should use a humble builder voice, showing judgment, process, and proof without generic authority-posturing.
Resolved style-default decision: candidate drafts should avoid emojis and hashtags by default unless the user explicitly asks for them.
Resolved detail-level decision: candidate drafts should prefer specific implementation details over broad strategic narrative, while explaining why the details matter.
Resolved quality-rubric decision: each candidate draft should include scores for credibility, voice match, evidence/provenance, specificity, and LinkedIn readability.
Resolved recommendation decision: the workflow should recommend one winning draft with rationale after generating and scoring candidate drafts.
Resolved red-team decision: before recommending a winner, the workflow should flag cringe, overclaiming, vague bragging, and unsupported hype.
Resolved audience-input decision: each draft run should require a target audience during grilling instead of silently inferring audience from selected context.
Resolved personalness decision: each draft run should ask for a low, medium, or high personalness level during grilling.
Resolved reader-action decision: draft runs should not require a desired reader action. The workflow may suggest one when useful, but it must not force every post into an explicit call to action.
Resolved length decision: the workflow should produce short-form LinkedIn drafts only, not long-form variants.
Resolved short-form length target: drafts should aim for 900-1300 characters with a hard maximum around 1500 characters.
Resolved input-mode decision: the workflow should support editing an existing rough draft as an input path, using the same voice, provenance, scoring, and red-team checks as generated drafts.

Resolved artifact-retention decision: completed draft runs should be stored as durable project artifacts, not only returned in chat, so they can support examples, voice-profile review, and regression fixtures.
Created PRD artifact for the LinkedIn post generation workflow and set next action to issue breakdown.
Created five issue artifacts from the PRD and set next action to implementation.
Saved the user-approved Philippine health insurance LinkedIn post draft as a draft-run artifact.
